claim_results.claim: Independent lines of evidence converging on the same structure — that convergence is the evidence.
Why the Pattern Is the Signal Independent lines of evidence converging on the same structure — that convergence is the evidence.

The death-resurrection-salvation arc appears across cultures because it is real — the physics of coherence restoration projected by the Logos — not because cultures fabricate it from a shared template.

Carrier's mythicist Bayesian argument fails on the reference class: treating pattern convergence as evidence of fabrication is a category error, like treating the universality of sunrise accounts as evidence the sun doesn't rise.
The framework doesn't just defend Jesus's historicity — it predicts it.

The strongest version of that argument comes from Richard Carrier , a historian who applied Bayes' Theorem — the same probabilistic logic used in medical diagnosis, criminal forensics, and signal processing — to the question of whether Jesus existed.
Not by dismissing Carrier's method — Bayes' Theorem is correct, and using it on historical questions is legitimate.
How Carrier's Argument Works The Bayesian equation is simple: posterior odds equal prior odds times the likelihood ratio.
You start with how likely something is before you look at the evidence, then you update based on what the evidence says.

Carrier's prior probability — his starting point before examining any evidence — comes from asking: in the set of all ancient figures most similar to Jesus, how often do they turn out to be historical rather than mythical?
He then examines four categories of evidence: extra-biblical sources, the book of Acts, the Gospels, and the Epistles.
Most of the evidence is equally likely whether Jesus existed or not (likelihood ratio of 1/1 — no effect).
Some evidence slightly favors mythicism.

The Reference Class Problem Carrier's entire prior — and therefore his entire conclusion — depends on one assumption: that the structural similarities between Jesus and other savior-hero figures (incarnation, death, resurrection, salvation, worshiped by followers) are evidence of fabrication .
Therefore Jesus was probably invented.
Rationality → Mathematics works The substrate is intelligible because the source is rational.
Self-consistency → Universal laws The same physics holds everywhere because the source is non-contradictory.
Conservation → Noether's theorem Symmetry yields conservation; the Logos is symmetric in its commitments.
Relationality → Four forces are couplings Reality is built of relations, not isolates — because the source is relational.
Triadic structure → Born Rule Probability requires three irreducible operations: prepare, evolve, measure.
If the Projection Principle is true, then the death-resurrection-salvation arc is not a mythological template that cultures copy from each other.

It shows up in Osiris and Hercules and Romulus and the Eleusinian mysteries for the same reason the conservation of energy shows up in every culture that does careful measurements: because it's there .

But if the pattern exists because reality actually has a death-resurrection-salvation architecture, then the class isn't "people who were fabricated using this template." The class is "people onto whom a real signal was projected." Those are different classes with different base rates.
The sunrise pattern appears everywhere because the sun actually rises.
The death-resurrection-salvation arc appears across cultures because the Logos projects it.
If we grant that the pattern is real — that reality actually contains a coherence-restoration mechanism — then we need different criteria for identifying the source instance.
Collectively, they are devastating to Carrier's conclusion — because his math depends on the evidence being weak.
The Framework's Additional Evidence This series adds something standard historical arguments don't have: a mechanism .
The framework predicts that consciousness coupling to the Logos field should produce measurable physical effects.
The physics predicts what happens if a perfectly Logos-coupled consciousness undergoes death within the temporal frame and the coherence field remains intact.
Carrier can't assign a vanishingly small prior to an event for which there is both a theoretical mechanism and experimental evidence of the underlying coupling.
The prior · 1/2 → 2/1 If the Projection Principle is correct, the convergence of the pattern across figures is evidence the pattern is real, not evidence of fabrication.
Extra-biblical evidence · 576/1250 → 1/1 Carrier's scoring depends on the assumption that the absence of stronger evidence is itself evidence for mythicism.

The framework argues the opposite: the evidence is exactly what we expect for a non-elite figure in an occupied territory whose movement was persecuted by the very institutions that preserved records.

The framework provides a reason: if what Paul experienced was the risen Christ through Logos-field coupling (which the PEAR-LAB data confirms is real), then Paul's emphasis on revelation over biography is exactly what we expect.
What We Are Not Claiming This article does not claim to have proven Jesus existed with mathematical certainty.
Bayes' Theorem produces probabilities, not proofs.
The claim is narrower and more precise: Carrier's reference class is contaminated by a real signal.
The death-resurrection-salvation pattern appears across cultures because it is projected by the Logos into every domain — not because cultures fabricate it from a shared template.
Treating the pattern as evidence of fabrication is a category error — like treating the universality of sunrise accounts as evidence the sun doesn't rise.

They follow from the Projection Principle, which this series has argued for across 10 articles with independent evidence from quantum mechanics, thermodynamics, information theory, and experimental consciousness research.
The framework doesn't just defend Jesus's historicity — it predicts it.
If the Logos is real, and the coherence-restoration arc is real, then the source event must have happened.
Not because theology requires it, but because the physics requires a source for the signal.
Carrier's entire prior depends on treating pattern convergence as evidence of fabrication.
If the pattern is real — which the Projection Principle argues with 10/10 confirmations — then the reference class is contaminated by signal.
Every model we construct is a projection of higher-dimensional reality onto a lower-dimensional surface we can comprehend.
We do not claim to have captured God in equations.
We claim that when we look at His creation honestly — with the tools of physics and the revelation of Scripture — the same structure appears in both.
Where our model limits what God can be, the limitation is ours, not His.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.
It started with the coherence equation and let the structure lead.
Independent convergence across formally distinct domains is the strongest evidence type in science.
The claim: The pattern IS the signal.

The degree of cross-domain convergence documented across this series — with falsifiable predictions, control group confirmation, and formal mathematical structure — is either the most elaborate coincidence in intellectual history or evidence that physics and theology are projections of a single underlying architecture.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.
Every equation that appears in the paper, collected here as a reference index.

Bayes' Theorem (Odds Form) $$\text{posterior odds} \;=\; \text{prior odds} \times \text{likelihood ratio}$$ The probability rule the article inherits from Carrier — the same logic used in medical diagnosis, criminal forensics, and signal processing.
Plain English: Start with how likely something is before evidence; multiply by how strongly the evidence cuts.
Carrier's method is correct; the article's claim is that one of his inputs — the prior, derived from a contaminated reference class — is wrong.

Corrected Posterior (All Three Fixes) $$P_{\text{hist}} = P\!\left[\tfrac{2}{1}\right] \times X[1] \times A\!\left[\tfrac{36}{50}\right] \times G[1] \times E^{\text{con}}\!\left[\tfrac{4}{5}\right] \times E^{\text{pro}}\!\left[\tfrac{8}{1}\right] = \tfrac{36.864}{1}$$ $$\Rightarrow \tfrac{36.864}{37.864} \approx 97.4\%$$ Three corrections: (1) prior 1/2 → 2/1 (Projection-Principle reference class), (2) extra-biblical 576/1250 → 1/1 (no-effect, conservative), (3) epistolary silences collapsed because the framework predicts them as Logos-coupling revelation.
Plain English: The framework can't claim a vanishing prior on the resurrection because the underlying coupling has experimental evidence at significance levels accepted in any branch of physics.

Projection Principle — Property Mapping $$\text{Logos property} \;\xrightarrow{\text{projects to}}\; \text{Physical-law property}$$ Six instances cited in this article: rationality → mathematics works; self-consistency → universal laws; conservation → Noether's theorem; relationality → four forces as couplings; triadic structure → Born Rule; coherence restoration → death-and-return arc.
Plain English: Sunrise analogy: 50 cultures describing a bright light in the east each morning is not evidence the sun was fabricated.
Bayes' Theorem A probability rule for updating belief in light of evidence.
Likelihood ratio = how strongly evidence cuts.
Carrier (Richard) Historian who applied Bayes' Theorem to Jesus's historicity ( On the Historicity of Jesus ).
The article's central claim: Carrier's class — “heavily mythologized savior heroes” — is contaminated by treating signal as noise.
Each property of the Logos shows up as an isomorphic property of the substrate.
Logos Field ($\chi$) The pervasive substrate of coherent reality — the framework's $\chi$ field; identified with the Master Equation's coherence field.
Same Master-Equation variable, two coordinate names: “entropy” in the physics frame, “sin” in the theological frame.
The shape that appears across cultures because reality has a coherence-restoration mechanism.
50 cultures describing a bright light in the east is not evidence of a shared fabrication; it is evidence the sun rises.
The article's diagnosis of Carrier's prior — treating pattern convergence as evidence of fabrication.
Same shape error as treating universal sunrise accounts as evidence the sun doesn't rise.
Carrier's E con evidence: things Paul doesn't mention about Jesus.
Grace (G) The negentropic input term in the coherence equation.

Cited as a Projection-Principle instance — three irreducible operations (prepare, evolve, measure / Father, Son, Spirit). (Defined in detail in Article 06.) Noether's Theorem Symmetry yields conservation.
